summary: Russian forces are using FPV drones to down a Ka-52 helicopter near Nadiivka, Donetsk, and strike the crew during evacuation, while also deploying a new 5 kg payload drone, the Klin, with 120 km range and autonomous targeting. The Rubicon Center reports over 20,000 enemy targets destroyed, with 35% being unmanned systems, and conducted 131 strikes on March 24 and 127 on March 25 across the Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman directions, targeting Ukrainian personnel, fixed-wing UAVs, and command centers. Ukrainian units like "Apachi" are countering Russian infiltration tactics on the Sloviansk front by detecting and destroying enemy shelters and positions in real time. Meanwhile, Iranian-backed militias have used fiber-optic FPV drones to attack U.S. Victory Base near Baghdad International Airport.
